infoPlist.NSFaceIDUsageDescription: Face ID approves a clinician's request to access your memory namespace for a single encounter. Your private key never leaves this device's Secure Enclave; Face ID only releases a time-limited wrapped key to the clinician.

NSAppTransportSecurity._comment: Demo-only: allow HTTP to the local relay (e.g. http://192.168.x.x:8080). For production at verum.sh/mae.sh: REMOVE this entire NSAppTransportSecurity block — HTTPS handles itself, and ATS is enabled by default.

ios._privacyManifests_comment: Apple requires PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy for App Store submission. We declare ZERO data collection and ZERO tracking — the relay only sees opaque age-encrypted blobs (not 'collected' per Apple's definition; see App Store Connect 'App Privacy' section). The four NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes are standard Required-Reasons API declarations for Expo SDK's typical usage; reason codes per https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/privacy_manifest_files/describing_use_of_required_reason_api . Expo SDK 54+ reads this block during `npx expo prebuild` and emits the actual ios/<App>/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy file.

extra._relayBaseUrl_comment: Override at runtime via EXPO_PUBLIC_RELAY_URL env var. Default is a placeholder LAN IP — set to the actual Sequoia local IP before building for the demo.
